description_long: This lantern features a traditional Asian-inspired design, with a pagoda-style roof that has upturned corners and a slightly weathered, metallic finish. The roof is supported by four slender posts, creating an open-sided chamber beneath, which would typically house a light source. The lantern sits atop a tall, ornate pedestal with intricate carvings and a square base, giving it a stately presence suitable for parks or garden pathways. The structure appears to be primarily made of metal for the roof and frame, with a stone or concrete pedestal. The roof shows signs of patina and wear, suggesting exposure to the elements. Decorative details are subtle, focusing on the elegant lines and form rather than elaborate ornamentation.
description: A tall, pagoda-style lantern with a weathered metal roof and ornate stone pedestal, featuring open sides and traditional Asian design elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full pagoda roof, open chamber, and detailed pedestal with a clear view of the square base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the roof's overhang and the vertical alignment of the supporting posts.
description_view_2: Angled view emphasizing the layered roof structure and the open space beneath, with visible weathering on the roof.
description_view_3: Rear angled view showing the back of the roof and pedestal, with some foliage or debris near the base.
